rulings.Case Title: IN RE:MARCIANA S. LIBRANO TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Modifying Trust And Appointing Trustee
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required. remote appearance highly encouraged. Please review the 6th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. ISSUES: 1) Petitioner is seeking to modify the trust to read: "In the event no named Trustees are available, a majority of the beneficiaries then eligible to receive mandatory or discretionary distributions of net income under this agreement shall forthwith name a successor Trustee. If the majority of the beneficiaries then eligible to receive mandatory or discretionary distributions of net income under this agreement cannot agree on a fiduciary, any beneficiary can petition a court of competent jurisdiction, ex parte, to designate a successor Trustee. 2) Whether to appoint Ronald S. Librano as trustee.

PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FIRST INITIAL AND LAST NAME. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JACK BARRY DANE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although, notwithstanding that when there is a waiver of account, if the petition and report requests statutory commissions or fees based on any amount other than the amount of the Inventory and Appraisal, CRC 7.705(b) requires detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses on sales to be included, and there were reportedly no receipts despite all cash allegedly being in interest bearing accounts.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HISAKO MINOBE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/04/2020 (no earlier date available). A supplement signed and verified by the petitioner addressing the following issues must be filed: 1) As the verification was signed in Hawaii, it must state "under penalty of perjury under the laws of the State of California". See CCP section 2015.5. 2) The date of issuance of letters stated in the supplement is still incorrect. 3) Paragraph 5 of the petition must include all the information required by CRC 7.403. A DE-174 form must also be filed to show how the petitioner has acted on the claim. 4) The Franchise Tax Board's address stated in the proof of service of notice to creditors appears different from the address designated by the Franchise Tax Board for purposes of Probate Code section 9202(c) notice. The petition must also allege the date of mailing. See LRSF 14.67(L)(2). 5) Why is the petitioner allowing one financial institution to hold funds far exceeding the FDIC limits? 6) All the information required by CRC 7.250(a) must be provided with respect to the sale of the estate real property and other IAEA actions taken by the petitioner without court approval for which notice of proposed action was required. 7) Please provide a copy of the final closing statement for the sale of the estate real property.

rulings.Case Title: **TRANSFERRED TO PLACER COUNTY** CONSERVATORSHIP OF ANGELA WAITE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/04/2020. 1) No Exhibits A & B are attached to the petition. 2) No bank statement verifying the ending account balance is on file. 3) The general conservators appear to have received $40,000, but the reported cash on hand at the end of the account period is $42,190.71. A supplemental account explaining the difference must be filed. 4) No proof of service of the inventory and appraisal is on file.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF ALAN LOUIS GREEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/04/2020. 1) An allegation required by CRC 7.703(e)(5) must be provided for the paralegal's services. 2) Is the total amount of costs set forth in paragraph 10 of the petition accurate? 3) Can the paralegal perform some of the additional services set forth in paragraph 11 of the petition? If so, should the $4,500 estimate be modified? 4) Paragraph 12 of the petition proposes transferring the estate to the San Francisco Public Administrator, but the proof of service shows notice was given to the San Mateo Public Administrator. 5) Has the petitioner contacted the VA to confirm the conservatee was not receiving benefits? 6) The summary of account must be on a mandatory Judicial Council form GC-400 (SUM). CRC 7.575(e)(5). 7) What happened to the tangible personal items recovered from Columbus Hotel? Have they been inventoried? 8) Did the petitioner as temporary conservator have authority to move the conservatee to Home Sweet Home care facility without court approval? The post-move notice is on file, but no pre-move notice has been filed. 9) Should the costs of $139.50 paid to the petitioner's attorneys on 10/29/2019 be charged to the estate, even though the costs were incurred to correct the error on the proposed order? 10) An original statement of the First Republic Bank CD account as of 11/05/2019 is not on file. 11) The petitioner's timesheets mention CALPERS Tax Withholding Election on 06/19/2019. Was the conservatee receiving CALPERS benefits? 12) Was there any valuable item in the conservatee's safety deposit box? 13) Should the petitioner be compensated for 0.6 hours of services related to the ex parte petition to correct the error on the proposed order? 14) What was the purpose of the petitioner's research on 10/10/2019? 15) Why did the petitioner need 6.4 hours to prepare the timesheets? A statement demonstrating the productivity of the time spent and other applicable factors under CRC 7.702 is needed. 16) Were the paralegal's services to research the conservatee's heirs on 12/05/2019 & 12/12/2019 duplicative of the research on 08/21/2019? 17) Why did the attorney and paralegal need 13.6 hours to prepare the final account? A statement demonstrating the productivity of the time spent and other applicable factors under CRC 7.702 is needed.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. No conformed copy of signed order will be returned (no self-addressed stamped envelope provided). Inventory and appraisal must be filed by 02/17/2021. Appearance required on 02/24/2021 if not filed. Petition for final distribution or petition for approval of report of status of administration must be filed by 01/19/2022. Appearance required on 01/26/2022 if not filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RAYMOND JAIR
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/4/2020. Issues: 1) Examiner does not have the required courtesy copies of the Objector's filed documents. File stamped single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. (LRSF 2.7, 14.6). 2) Examiner does not have a proposed order from Petitioner. A hard copy of the proposed order must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. (LRSF 14.93). [Proposed orders may not be e-filed.] 3) Petitioner has not filed Duities and Liabilities.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STEPHEN CHAPMAN MATCHETT
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Petition could have been pre-granted but for the request for express authorizations that are already included under IAEA or with Notice of Proposed Action. (The attachment to the proposed order has a further authorization regarding hiring and paying a landlord tenant attorney, which is also not appropriate and was not disclosed in the petition.) Please review the 6th amended general order on the court's website re the strong preference for all attorneys and parties to appear remotely as a result of the COVID-19 emergency and for information on appearing by CourtCall or via Zoom. (A 7th amended general order is imminent. Parties should check the Court's website closer to the hearing date for further instructions regarding remote appearance.) For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.
